in a hospital, would not result in a different finding than that of murder,

is there any reason to alter the sentence imposed.
5. 
We have gone through the entire records and depositions of the 
witnesses. At the outset, we have to notice that the ocular witnesses 
were all grandchildren of the deceased; which by itself would not 
result in eschewing their testimony. It is trite that, merely because 
witnesses are related, they cannot be termed to the interested,
